title: hieracium praecox subsp. foetidum
intro: Hieracium praecox subsp. foetidum, also known as Foetid Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Foetid Hawkweed include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used in infusions or teas, and it can also be dried and made into capsules or tinctures for easier consumption.
